title: Suregada philippinensis
taxonomy: Suregada philippinensis is a species of plant in the family Euphorbiaceae. It belongs to the genus Suregada, which is a group of flowering plants native to tropical Asia. The Euphorbiaceae family consists of over 300 genera and 7,000 species of plants. Suregada philippinensis is often found in forests and woodlands, where it grows as a small tree or shrub. This plant is native to the Philippines and surrounding regions.
uses: Suregada philippinensis is known to have various medicinal uses in traditional medicine. Its bark and leaves are used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other ailments. The plant is also said to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help in reducing pain and swelling. In some cultures, it is used to treat digestive issues and as a diuretic. Further research is needed to confirm the effectiveness and safety of using Suregada philippinensis for medicinal purposes.

The health benefits of this plant include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which are thought to aid in the treatment of various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections.
